methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the official UCLouvain AI portal, official documents page, student guidance PDF, OER working-group PDF, and course-specific material.
Public web search confirmed the UCLouvain /fr/ia section as the strongest official-source path after a later Firecrawl query returned an insufficient-credits error.
robots.txt disallowed admin/login/search-style paths but not the public AI pages or the official student PDF. Direct HTTP fetches returned HTTP 200.
The OER repository did not expose a robots.txt file and the official working-group PDF fetched successfully.
The official documents page linked faculty-specific SharePoint/restricted pages. Those were recorded as rejected or inaccessible rather than used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.summary: Official UCLouvain sources support narrow French-language claims about the university AI documents hub, student guidance principles, recommended student tools, Piccolo access and data handling, and teaching/evaluation recommendations from the 2024 working-group PDF. Faculty-specific SharePoint or restricted pages and course-specific pages were not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Lorsqu’une activité d’apprentissage est proposée aux étudiant·es, il faut être explicite sur l’utilisation possible de l’IA générative : Sont-ils permis ou interdits ? ... artifacts.title: Université catholique de Louvain (UCLouvain) AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Official French-language UCLouvain sources found in this staged crawl support narrow claims about an official AI documents hub, student guidance based on responsibility/authenticity/transparency, recommended student tools, Piccolo access and stated data handling, and 2024 working-group recommendations for explicit teaching/evaluation rules and caution around AI detectors. Faculty-specific restricted or SharePoint sources and a course-only PDF were rejected or marked inaccessible.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The 2024 working-group note was treated as recommendations and not as binding regulation.
Faculty-specific restricted or SharePoint guidance was not accessed or used for university-wide claims.
Course-specific AI-use language was rejected and not generalized.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
